---
title: Extensions
llms-description: Extending Inspect with new Model APIs, tool execution environments, and storage platforms.
---

## Overview

There are several ways to extend Inspect to integrate with systems not directly supported by the core package:

| | |
|---|---|
| [Model APIs](extensions-model-api.qmd) | Model hosting services, local inference engines, etc. |
| [Components](extensions-components.qmd) | Tasks, solvers, scorers, and tools distributed in packages. |
| [Sandboxes](extensions-sandboxes.qmd) | Local or cloud container runtimes for tool execution. |
| [Approvers](extensions-approvers.qmd) | Approve, modify, or reject tool calls. |
| [Hooks](extensions-hooks.qmd) | Logging and monitoring lifecycle hooks. |
| [Filesystems](extensions-filesystems.qmd) | Storage for datasets, prompts, and evaluation logs. |
: {.borderless tbl-colwidths=[35,65]}

For each of these, you can create an extension within a Python package, and then use it without any special registration with Inspect (this is done via [setuptools entry points](https://setuptools.pypa.io/en/latest/userguide/entry_point.html)).
